title: Primula × schinzii
intro: Primula × schinzii, commonly known as the Drachenblut or Dragon's Blood Primrose, is a hybrid plant species in the Primulaceae family, characterized by its unique, blood-red colored flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever and soothing respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Primula × schinzii include flavonoids, saponins, and iridoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Primula × schinzii is a hybrid species within the Primulaceae family. It is a cross between two different species of Primula. As a hybrid, Primula × schinzii does not fit neatly into the typical binomial classification system. Instead, it is often classified as a cultivar or a hybrid species within the Primula genus. The exact classification and naming conventions for this hybrid can vary depending on the source.

Some traditional uses of Primula × schinzii include treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. However, more research is needed to confirm its potential medicinal benefits and determine safe dosages.
